prompt:
Vertical 9:16 cinematic devotional oil painting video clip, slower pacing with longer holds and restrained cuts.

Classical devotional oil painting realism. A faint cold light rests on a pale central figure on the cross while the land and sky drop into unnatural black noon, a total-eclipse sun ringed in the dark; visible brushwork, craquelure, aged varnish throughout.

Scene contains:
Three wooden crosses stand on a dark hill beneath a black noon sky, the central crucified Christ pale at the heart of it, nailed and head bowed. A ringed eclipse sun hangs in the black sky; mourning and fleeing figures are on the road below, a mounted centurion at the side, and shadowed city walls in the murk. The scene is a painted tableau — a frozen moment, not an action. Nothing moves; this is a still painting.

Scene:
Start on full composition — three crosses on the dark hill under the black sky.

Cut to mid framing — the central crucified Christ.

Cut to insert — the ringed eclipse sun in the black sky.

Cut to close-up — the central figure's bowed head.

Cut to lower framing — the mourning figures on the road.

Cut to insert — the mounted centurion at the side.

Cut to upper framing — the shadowed city walls in the murk.

Return to full composition.

End hold on the central crucified figure amid the darkness.

Micro-motion:
- minimal

Camera:
- crop-based reframing only
- no invented movement

Emotion:
Vast, cosmic desolation.

No added elements.

10.0s

9:16
negative_prompt: no camera invention, no zoom invented, no pan invented, no push-in invented, no dolly, no tracking, no rotation, no animated subject motion, no figures moving, no morphing, no scene morphing, no facial morphing, no glow added, no halo added, no light rays added beyond what is painted, no atmospheric haze, no scene enhancement, no divine effects added, no smooth transitions, no motion blur, no reinterpretation, no new elements, no text, no watermarks, no extra limbs, no flickering, no warping, no animation, no action, no subject performs any task, no body motion, no limbs moving, the painting is fixed and frozen, only the camera reframes within the still image
